metadata.title: Build an Agentic Prompt Handoff Auditor with Claude

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: I'm chaining prompts where one step's output feeds the next, and things quietly break at the seams. I want to build a handoff auditor. Help me: (1) define what the auditor should check when output from step one becomes input to step two — missing context, dropped constraints, ambiguous references; (2) write the auditor prompt that flags each broken handoff before the chain runs; (3) suggest how it should report a failure so I know exactly which slot to repair.

props.command: I'm chaining prompts where one step's output feeds the next, and things quietly break at the seams. I want to build a handoff auditor. Help me: (1) define what the auditor should check when output from step one becomes input to step two — missing context, dropped constraints, ambiguous references; (2) write the auditor prompt that flags each broken handoff before the chain runs; (3) suggest how it should report a failure so I know exactly which slot to repair.
props.segment: Build an Agentic Prompt Handoff Auditor

beats.label: Hook — handoff failures
beats.narration: Multi-step agentic prompts fail at handoffs — the boundary between what one step produces and what the next step expects. That's where the chain breaks. And it's almost never the handoff you thought was obvious.

shots.notes: Flow diagram: Step 1 -> HANDOFF -> Step 2. HANDOFF box highlighted in CRIMSON with label 'format mismatch: numbered list vs JSON array'. Step 2 box has CRIMSON X — 'fails here'. Teardown palette.

beats.label: Claude command
beats.narration: The audit command reads a multi-step agentic chain and classifies each handoff as compatible, incompatible, or ambiguous — and flags all incompatible and ambiguous transitions.

props.command: claude "Audit the handoffs in this multi-step agentic prompt chain. For each handoff N->N+1: Step N output format, Step N+1 input expectation, classify: compatible/incompatible/ambiguous. Flag all incompatible and ambiguous." < prompt_chain.md

beats.label: Python code
beats.narration: The script reads the prompt chain, runs the handoff audit through Claude, and outputs a compatibility matrix showing each handoff's status.

props.code:
import subprocess, sys
chain = open(sys.argv[1]).read()
audit_cmd = (
  "Audit handoffs in this multi-step agentic chain. "
  "For each N->N+1: output format, input expectation, "
  "classify: compatible/incompatible/ambiguous. "
  "Flag all incompatible and ambiguous handoffs."
)
result = subprocess.run(
  ["claude", audit_cmd],
  input=chain, capture_output=True, text=True
)
print(result.stdout)

beats.label: Handoff compatibility matrix
beats.narration: The audit shows three handoffs: Step 1 to 2 is compatible. Step 2 to 3 is incompatible — numbered list output into a JSON array expectation. Step 3 to 4 is ambiguous.

shots.notes: Three-row matrix. Columns: HANDOFF | OUTPUT FORMAT | INPUT EXPECTED | STATUS. Row 1: 1->2 | markdown paragraphs | text block | COMPATIBLE (green). Row 2: 2->3 | numbered list | JSON array | INCOMPATIBLE (crimson). Row 3: 3->4 | unstructured text | structured fields | AMBIGUOUS (gold). Teardown palette.

beats.label: The change — fix incompatible handoff
beats.narration: Fix the incompatible handoff by adding an explicit output format specification to Step 2 — 'output as JSON array with keys: title, summary, confidence.' Re-run the audit.

props.command: claude "Audit handoffs again after Step 2 now specifies: output as JSON array with keys title/summary/confidence." < prompt_chain_fixed.md

beats.label: Changed output
beats.narration: Handoff 2 to 3: incompatible turns compatible. The arrow turns green. One format specification fixed the chain's weakest link.

shots.notes: BEFORE: row 2->3 — INCOMPATIBLE, crimson arrow, 'numbered list vs JSON array.' AFTER: row 2->3 — COMPATIBLE, green arrow, 'JSON array matches expectation.' Single row before/after comparison. Caption: 'One format spec closes the gap.'

beats.label: Teardown angle
beats.narration: Agentic chains are only as reliable as their weakest handoff — and the weakest handoff is never the one you think is obvious. It's the one where format was left to interpretation.

shots.notes: Large text: 'Weakest handoff = format left to interpretation.' Below: 'Spec the output contract at every step.' CRIMSON on 'left to interpretation.' Teardown palette, CRIMSON rule.

beats.label: Next reel teaser
beats.narration: Next: a GIGO detector for data prompts — make the invisible assumptions visible.

shots.notes: Clean: 'NEXT: Data GIGO Detector →' Minimal teardown style.
